_skill.name: lap_annotation
_skill.description: Per-label telemetry characteristics for the 8 candidate parent labels (EA, MSP, MSR, RM, PS, O, OD, MD) that the lap-to-segment excerpter can attach to one circuit-section-anchored range. Each label declares its telemetry signature (trajectory_offset, time_difference_to_expert slope, brake overlap, opponent context, etc.) and the global_rules block prescribes the detection procedure. Source of truth for "does this range qualify as MSP / RM / EA / O / OD / MSR ... at all?" The circuit + circuit_section labels are picked by the LLM via the `get_circuit_id` / `locate_circuit_section` tools (geometry from `app/domain/circuit_sections.py`). Solo sessions are rough-cut by circuit section; sessions with opponent data emit only close overtake offence / defense engagement windows.
when_to_use: Lap excerpter assigning one parent label per circuit section.
Building the system prompt for the lap-flow planner/synthesizer.
Evaluator checks for parent-label justification against telemetry.

EA.name: Expert Adherence
EA.characteristics:
Player tracks the expert through the section.
  - `trajectory_offset` stays within ±0.5 m of zero for the whole range.
  - `time_difference_to_expert` slope is ≈ 0 (gap neither grows nor
    shrinks) — confirm with `compute_slope`.
  - Speed delta at sampled ilocs is small.
Mutually exclusive with MSP and RM.

MSP.name: Mistake (Practice)
MSP.characteristics:
Technical driving error causing time loss or instability, NOT caused by interaction with a close opponent.
  - `time_difference_to_expert` rises continuously (gap grows) — confirm
    with `compute_slope` (positive) and `find_extremum` (max - min > 0).
  - 'time_difference_to_expert' slope rises at the end of the corner exit, this
    may indicate a mistake.
  - `trajectory_offset` exceeds the expert envelope: a spike outward
    (ran wide) or inward (cut tight) that does NOT recover within the
    section.
  - In opponent-capable data, `classify_opponent_interaction(start, end)`
    does NOT return an adverse racing outcome (`failed_attack` or
    `broken_defense`). A close opponent + adverse classifier outcome ⇒ MSR.
Mutually exclusive with EA, RM, MSR. The detailed-annotation flow refines MSP sub-ranges later — attach MSP at the section level when the section is dominated by a technical error.

MSR.name: Mistake (Racing)
MSR.characteristics:
Time / position loss caused by an interaction with a close opponent inside the section.
  - Call `classify_opponent_interaction(start, end)`. Require
    `outcome` = `failed_attack` or `broken_defense`,
    `gates.MSR: true`, and `label_gates.MSR: true`. Low / weak
    confidence means revise the range or inspect opponent trajectory before
    labeling.
  - Use `find_nearest_opponent` / `query_opponent_trajectory` only as
    supporting evidence for the primary slot and technique.
  - `time_difference_to_expert` typically grows across the
    interaction window.
Mutually exclusive with EA, RM, MSP. No close opponent or classifier outcome not in {failed_attack, broken_defense} ⇒ not MSR.

RM.name: Recovery & Merge
RM.characteristics:
Player recovering from a prior mistake inside this section.
  - `time_difference_to_expert` slope is negative across the range
    (gap shrinks, never grows) — confirm with `compute_slope`.
  - `trajectory_offset` trends back toward zero from a non-zero start.
Often appears in the section immediately following an MSP. Do NOT attach when the player started at offset ≈ 0 — that's EA, not RM. Mutually exclusive with EA and MSP.

PS.name: Pit Stop
PS.characteristics:
Player entering or sitting in the pit lane. Merging back out from the pit toward the expert line is RM, not PS.
  - `distance_to_expert_line` magnitude persistently large
    (≥ ~5 m) AND NOT trending toward zero (a decreasing offset
    ⇒ merging out ⇒ RM, not PS).
Picking which named catalog section to attach is a sub-label decision — see `sub_label_annotation`. Compatible with the circuit label only; do NOT combine with EA / MSP / MSR / RM / O / OD.

O.name: Successful Overtake
O.characteristics:
Player gained a position on a specific opponent inside the section. Multi-car positional data (`Car_{1..60}_pos_{x,y,z}`) is the gate.
  - Call `classify_opponent_interaction(start, end)`. Require
    `outcome: pass_completed`, `gates.O: true`, and `label_gates.O: true` —
    the deterministic signed longitudinal relationship says the opponent
    was ahead at entry and behind by exit. Empty / no-data / non-pass
    outcomes ⇒ do NOT attach O. Low / weak confidence means revise the range
    or inspect opponent trajectory before labeling.
  - Player-trace evidence to ALSO hold: late-brake signature
    past the expert reference, sustained outside / inside line
    deviation NOT explained by MSP / RM, or speed > expert on
    equal-or-less throttle (draft).
A close opponent + failed attack is MSR at the section level — sub-labelled `MSR1` in the detailed flow. A successful defense is OD, not O.

OD.name: Successful Defense
OD.characteristics:
Player held position against a real, close opponent inside the section.
  - Call `classify_opponent_interaction(start, end)`. Require
    `outcome: held_defense`, `gates.OD: true`, and `label_gates.OD: true` —
    the deterministic relationship says the opponent threatened from behind
    / alongside but did not get ahead by exit. A merely close car with no
    held-defense outcome is not OD. Low / weak confidence means revise the
    range or inspect opponent trajectory before labeling.
  - Player-trace evidence to ALSO hold: brake initiation
    earlier than expert and/or trajectory tighter than expert
    (inside cover), or a throttle lift on a straight with no
    brake onset (defensive lift). Time delta need not be flat
    — it may grow briefly during the defense as long as the
    position holds.
A defense that failed (opponent got past) is MSR at the section level — sub-labelled `MSR2` in the detailed flow.

MD.name: Missing data
MD.characteristics:
Section contains telemetry gaps, dropouts, or corrupted values.
  - A signal that should be dynamic flatlines (e.g. speed = 0 mid-lap).
  - Physically impossible values (negative speed, gear out of range).
  - NaN runs inside the section.
Trigger: a `find_extremum` returns an impossible min/max, or a rendered graph shows a flat plateau where motion should exist. Compatible with every other label.

global_rules:
Detection procedure. The splitter handed
you a rough iloc window anchored to a circuit section. Solo sessions are
processed as normal driving sections. Sessions with opponent data are
processed only as close overtake offence / defense engagement windows; for
those ranges, pick O for a successful attacking pass, OD for a held defense,
or MSR for a failed attack / broken defense. The deterministic gate for these
three labels is `classify_opponent_interaction(start, end)`: O requires
`outcome: pass_completed`, OD requires `outcome: held_defense`, and MSR
requires `outcome` in {`failed_attack`, `broken_defense`}. Prefer labels only
when the matching confidence-aware `label_gates` value is true. Low / weak
classifier confidence means revise the range, inspect `query_opponent_trajectory`,
or submit an empty label list if the evidence remains unclear. The classifier
uses expert-trajectory projection for signed longitudinal / lateral gaps when
expert positions exist, and reports `coordinate_frame` so you can cite whether
it used `expert_path_projection` or fallback `player_heading_projection`. If
the classifier does not support one of those outcomes, submit an empty label
list rather than labeling EA / MSP / RM / PS / MD. Label which circuit + named
circuit_section it is by calling `get_circuit_id` and `locate_circuit_section`
(geometry from `app/domain/circuit_sections.py`) and include both ids in your
`label_ids`.
When `locate_circuit_section` reports `is_ambiguous`, disambiguate from
`top_matches` with a second signal (pit-limiter speed, persistent lateral
offset, brake pattern) rather than guessing. Then pick the parent main label
(+ optional ST1–ST6, + optional sub-labels).

1. **Render the two diagnostic graphs.** One `render_graph` each over
   the current iloc range:
     - `trajectory_offset` — discriminates EA vs. MSP vs. RM vs. PS in solo
       sessions and helps read inside / outside cover for O / OD / MSR.
     - `time_difference_to_expert` — confirms gap direction (flat ⇒ EA,
       rising ⇒ MSP / MSR, falling ⇒ RM).
   Add brake / throttle / speed or opponent tools when a specific O / OD /
   MSR / MD candidate needs it.

2. **Score each candidate label against its `characteristics` block.**
   Use `query_telemetry` for quantitative confirmation:
     - `compute_slope` on `time_difference_to_expert` → sign and magnitude
       of the gap trend.
     - `find_extremum` on `trajectory_offset` → peak deviation from zero.
     - `find_first_match` on `Physics_brake` / `Physics_gas` → onset ilocs
       when O / OD / MSR needs late-brake, inside-cover, or defensive-lift
       evidence.
   For O / OD / MSR candidates call `classify_opponent_interaction(start, end)`
   over the FULL current range and treat its `outcome` / `gates` /
   `label_gates` as the mathematical label eligibility:
     - O requires `outcome: pass_completed`, `gates.O: true`, and
       `label_gates.O: true`.
     - OD requires `outcome: held_defense`, `gates.OD: true`, and
       `label_gates.OD: true`.
     - MSR requires `outcome: failed_attack` or `broken_defense`,
       `gates.MSR: true`, and `label_gates.MSR: true`.
   Use `find_nearest_opponent` and `query_opponent_trajectory(start, end,
   slot, n_samples)` for supporting primary-slot details and technique.
   No supported classifier outcome ⇒ rule out all three (O / OD / MSR).

3. **Refine the boundary for parent-label fit.** The characteristics blocks
   assert their signature across the WHOLE range. If the picked main label
   fits only part of the rough window, the engagement starts before the range,
   the pass resolves after the range, or classifier confidence is low / weak,
   call `revise_range` to shrink or extend so ONE signature holds uniformly.
   The working envelope is the lap range, so outward extension is allowed.
   Re-render the diagnostic graphs and rerun `classify_opponent_interaction`
   on the new range before scoring again.

4. **Optionally pick one ST1–ST6** from the label catalog descriptions when
   the trajectory shape is unambiguous. Use `compute_expert_phases` to help:
   empty phases ⇒ straight (ST2), one arc ⇒ in/approach/exit corner
   (ST1/ST3/ST4), multi-arc with no flat gap ⇒ ST6. Skip the ST pick entirely
   when the shape is ambiguous.

5. **Submit** via `submit_result` with `label_ids = [circuit id,
   circuit_section id, ...optional ST, ...optional main labels]` and a
   `reasoning` string citing the ilocs / slopes / offset peaks / classifier
   outcome / confidence level / opponent slot fields you observed.

Hard rules:
  - One proposal per agent run; the UI loops one section per click.
  - Never invent label IDs — only IDs from the eligible list are accepted.
  - In opponent sessions, do not attach EA / MSP / RM / PS / MD; use O / OD /
    MSR or submit `[]`.
  - EA + MSP, EA + MSR, EA + RM, MSP + MSR, MSP + RM, MSR + RM are all
    contradictions — pick at most one from {EA, MSP, MSR, RM}.
  - O + OD, O + MSR, OD + MSR are all contradictions — pick at most one
    opponent-aware label per range.
  - PS is incompatible with EA / MSP / RM / O / OD.
  - Empty `label_ids` `[]` is the valid "drop this section" signal.
  - Final iloc range must remain inside the lap and ≥ 3 ilocs.
